working: Staff-Client eNews - May 2026 (5/14) remains the top performer: +76.5% traffic lift over baseline (17.0 -> 30.0 daily sessions) on 9,731 sends at a 48.2% open rate.
Alumni eNews: Final Reminder - June 2026 (6/3) lifted traffic +65.2% (21.0 -> 34.7 daily sessions) at 45.9% open, the most recent send in-window and proof the reminder cadence keeps working.
Open rates stay excellent: 52.15% average across 100 campaigns, well above the 25-35% health/nonprofit benchmark.
Alumni eNews - May 2026 (5/2) had the best engagement quality of the in-window sends: 1.79% click rate and 3.77% click-to-open.
Organic Search holds 36.4% share (795 of 2,184 sessions), the #2 channel and BCC's primary growth lane behind inflated Direct.
not_working: AUTOMATION GAP: the scheduled fetch did not run today (the claude-runner host process has been offline since 6/11), so no GA4/CC/GSC data refreshed for this 6/15 cycle; figures below are carried from the 6/11 fetch.

Total UTM leakage persists: all 12 in-window sends show email_sessions_3d = 0 while logging 21-76 direct_sessions_3d each, so every newsletter click is mis-credited to Direct (55.5% of all traffic) because links carry no UTM tags.
Clicks lag opens badly: 2.07% average click rate and click-to-open mostly 1-2% (benchmark ~10%) - subscribers open but content is not driving site visits.
Several recent sends produced negative lift: Alumni eNews - May 2026 -50.2%, Reminder 2: Alumni eNews - March -31.1%, Movement Reminder: Staff-Client March -26.7%, Alumni eNews - June (5/27) -9.8%.
Organic Social is dormant at 1.6% (35 sessions) and AI Assistant is 1 session; GSC shows only 4 clicks on 1,706 impressions (0.23% CTR) at impression-weighted position 18.8.
do_next: Restart the claude-runner host process (node runner.js / start.bat) so the twice-weekly fetch + publish resume; today's queued fetch/publish jobs are waiting and the deployed dashboard has not updated since 6/11.

Add utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email (plus utm_campaign per send) to every newsletter link - the #1 measurement fix; it reclassifies the email portion of the 55.5% Direct bucket and makes lift trustworthy.

Keep the mid-week reminder pattern: best lifts came from a Thursday Staff-Client send (5/14, +76.5%) and a Wednesday final-reminder (6/3, +65.2%); weekend/Monday alumni openers underperformed (5/2 -50.2%).
Repromote Staff-Client themes: both lift leaders were Staff-Client or its reminder, and the ~9.7K Staff-Client list converts attention to visits better than the ~1K alumni list.
SEO follow-up: impression-weighted position sits at 18.8 with only 4 clicks; prioritize on-page work for the 23 tracked keywords with data to push page-2 pages toward page 1.
summary: Today's automated fetch did not run because the claude-runner host process is offline (last activity 6/11), so this cycle reuses the 6/11 data: a 90-day window of 2,184 sessions led by Direct (55.5%, inflated by untagged newsletter clicks) and Organic Search (36.4%). Email opens stay strong (52% avg) and the 5/14 Staff-Client and 6/3 reminder sends drove +77%/+65% lifts, but ~2% click rates and a slipping GSC position cap upside. Top priority: restart the runner and add UTM tags to newsletter links.
